original: Agathon counsels keeping the mind of a stranger among the brethren.
english: Community decays when wonder becomes careless familiarity.
spanish: La comunidad se deteriora cuando el asombro se vuelve familiaridad descuidada.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 17. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon says he never slept with a grievance, nor let another sleep with one if he could help it.
english: Do not carry a wound into sleep if it can be healed before nightfall.
spanish: No lleves una herida al sueño si puede sanar antes de la noche.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, pp. 17-18. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon accepts accusations against himself but refuses separation from God.
english: Insults can be medicine; separation from the source is not.
spanish: Los insultos pueden ser medicina; la separación de la fuente no lo es.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 5
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 18. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon compares bodily discipline to foliage and interior vigilance to fruit.
english: Leaves protect the tree, but the fruit tells whether the life has ripened.
spanish: Las hojas protegen el árbol, pero el fruto dice si la vida maduró.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 8
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 18. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon says no labor is greater than prayer, a struggle until the last breath.
english: Prayer is not rest from struggle; it is the struggle kept open to God.
spanish: La oración no es descanso de la lucha; es la lucha mantenida abierta a Dios.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 9
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, pp. 18-19. Short working rendering.

artifact: Agathon 11 — Did You Put It There?
original: Agathon asks whether a brother put down the thing he now wants to take.
english: Before taking, ask whether your hand also bore the placing.
spanish: Antes de tomar, pregunta si tu mano también cargó el poner.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 11
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 19. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon keeps a stone in his mouth until he learns silence.
english: When the tongue will not learn, the body can become its teacher.
spanish: Cuando la lengua no aprende, el cuerpo puede volverse su maestro.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 15
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 19. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon tells himself not to judge what he sees.
english: A recollected mind begins where judgment is told: this is not yours.
spanish: Una mente recogida empieza donde al juicio se le dice: esto no es tuyo.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 18
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 20. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon says an angry person would not be acceptable even if he performed wonders.
english: A miracle cannot sanctify the anger that carries it.
spanish: Un milagro no puede santificar la ira que lo porta.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 19
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 20. Short working rendering.

original: Agathon imagines taking another's suffering onto himself as perfect charity.
english: Perfect charity imagines no boundary even at the skin.
spanish: La caridad perfecta no imagina frontera ni siquiera en la piel.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 26
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 20. Short working rendering.

original: At death Agathon says he has no confidence until he meets God.
english: Even a life of effort does not presume to grade itself before the final meeting.
spanish: Ni una vida de esfuerzo presume calificarse antes del encuentro final.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Agathon 29
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 21. Short working rendering.

original: Ammonas refuses extreme alternatives and counsels staying in the cell with the publican's word in the heart.
english: Do not flee into spectacle; remain small, steady, and accused before mercy.
spanish: No huyas hacia el espectáculo; permanece pequeño, firme y acusado ante la misericordia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Ammonas 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 22. Short working rendering.

original: Ammonas waits for the ordinary ferry rather than crossing by a private boat.
english: Peace sometimes means refusing the shortcut that agitates the spirit.
spanish: La paz a veces significa rechazar el atajo que agita el espíritu.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Ammonas 6
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 23. Short working rendering.

original: Anthony teaches Ammonas to become like a stone that does not answer blows.
english: Goodness becomes strong when accusation finds no hook to pull it into punishment.
spanish: La bondad se vuelve fuerte cuando la acusación no encuentra gancho para arrastrarla al castigo.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Ammonas 8
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 23. Short working rendering.

original: Ammonas protects a brother from exposure and then privately says: be on guard.
english: Mercy covers the person while still speaking directly to the wound.
spanish: La misericordia cubre a la persona sin dejar de hablarle directamente a la herida.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Ammonas 10
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 24. Short working rendering.

original: Ammonas says the narrow way is to control thoughts and strip away one's own will for God.
english: The narrow road is not far away; it passes through thought and self-will.
spanish: El camino estrecho no está lejos; atraviesa el pensamiento y la voluntad propia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Ammonas 11
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 24. Short working rendering.

original: Achilles gives to the brother with a bad reputation so his soul will not be overcome with grief.
english: The bruised soul sometimes needs kindness before correction can be heard.
spanish: El alma golpeada a veces necesita bondad antes de poder oír corrección.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Achilles 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 24. Short working rendering.

original: Achilles prays until the painful word becomes like blood he can spit out and forget.
english: A word not spoken in anger may still have to be bled out in prayer.
spanish: Una palabra no dicha con ira quizá aún tenga que sangrarse en oración.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Achilles 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 25. Short working rendering.

original: Ammoe sends away a brother after seven days, saying his sins are a dark well between him and God.
english: Sometimes the word given is the elder's confession that no word can yet pass through.
spanish: A veces la palabra dada es la confesión del anciano de que aún ninguna palabra puede pasar.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Ammoe 4
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 26. Short working rendering.

original: Anoub teaches the brothers to live together like a statue unmoved by blows or flattery.
english: Common life begins when praise and injury stop deciding the weather inside.
spanish: La vida común empieza cuando elogio y herida dejan de decidir el clima interior.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anoub 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 28. Short working rendering.

original: Abraham distinguishes destroyed passions from passions still living but controlled.
english: A chained force is not destroyed; it is only held by vigilance.
spanish: Una fuerza encadenada no está destruida; sólo está sostenida por vigilancia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Abraham 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 29. Short working rendering.

original: A scribe says to practice what is already written before asking for the missing lines.
english: The unwritten part waits until the written part has entered the hands.
spanish: La parte no escrita espera hasta que la escrita haya entrado en las manos.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Abraham 3
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 29. Short working rendering.

original: Alonios says peace requires saying in the heart: in the world there is only myself and God.
english: Peace begins when the crowd inside is reduced to one soul before God.
spanish: La paz empieza cuando la multitud interior se reduce a un alma ante Dios.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Alonios 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 30. Short working rendering.

artifact: Apphy 1 — Grace in Solitude and World
original: Apphy learns that what was possible in solitude changes when human help surrounds him.
english: Some austerity belongs to solitude; some weakness is exposed by being among people.
spanish: Cierta austeridad pertenece a la soledad; cierta debilidad se revela entre la gente.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Apphy 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, pp. 30-31. Short working rendering.

original: Apollo sets out for work joyfully, saying he goes to work with Christ today.
english: The day's labor changes when it is received as work beside Christ.
spanish: El trabajo del día cambia cuando se recibe como labor junto a Cristo.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Apollo 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 31. Short working rendering.

original: Bessarion refuses anxious hoarding after sea water becomes drinkable.
english: The miracle is lost when fear immediately tries to store it.
spanish: El milagro se pierde cuando el miedo intenta almacenarlo de inmediato.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Bessarion 1
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 34. Short working rendering.

artifact: Bessarion 7 — I Too Am a Sinner
original: When a brother is turned out, Bessarion rises and goes with him.
english: The one cast out is not alone when another remembers his own need for mercy.
spanish: El expulsado no está solo cuando otro recuerda su propia necesidad de misericordia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Bessarion 7
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 35. Short working rendering.

original: Bessarion tells a brother sharing lodging: keep silence and do not compare yourself with others.
english: Comparison is a noise; silence is the room where the work can continue.
spanish: La comparación es un ruido; el silencio es la habitación donde el trabajo puede continuar.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Bessarion 10
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 35. Short working rendering.

original: Bessarion says at death that the monk ought to be all eye.
english: At the end, the whole person is asked to become attention.
spanish: Al final, a la persona entera se le pide volverse atención.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Bessarion 11
notes: Mode A completion queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 35. Short working rendering.
